tweets.full_text:
▶画像・資料を作るなら → GPT
　image2と5.5で精度が別物になりました

▶投稿やコンテンツを書くなら → Claude
　日本語が自然で、GPTみたいなくどさがない

▶コードを書くなら → Codex × Claude Code 併用
　片方が制限来ても、もう片方に切り替え
　→開発が止まらない最強環境になります

迷うより、まず使い分けてみてください。

tweets.full_text:
【Claude Codeに投げた言葉】(2/5)

ターミナルで日本語でこう頼んだ。

「部内研修用のウェブページを作ってください。

　内容：生成AIの基本的な使い方・
　　　　職場で使えるツールの紹介・
　　　　実際のプロンプト例

　条件：
　・スマホで読みやすいデザイン
　・セクションごとに区切る
　・シンプルで見やすい構成」

コードの中身はわからなかったけど、
30分後には動くページができてた。

「あ、これで行ける」と思った瞬間だった。

tweets.full_text:
色々と考えていたことがあり、Claude CodeやCoworkの利用者像をいろいろと調べた感じ、切っていいなという判断。Claude4.7の登場であらゆる面でのAnthropicのアドが失われている感じだ。

Claude CodeもCoworkもBiz系の人達が主に使っており（企業導入など）、その周辺で仕事をしている人も使わざるを得ないという感じ。ミトスの話題もあって現在も導入は増えているだろう。

エンジニア層では、Claudeは話題にも上らないという場面が増えている（Geminiと同じ道）。みんなCodex+5.5に夢中だ。
ただ、半年後にはCursor＆X が台頭していそう。たぶん。

そんな感じなので、私は無理にClaude CodeやCoworkを使う必要もないなという感じ。Opus4.6とSonnet4.6はAPIで使うけど。

tweets.full_text:
Get every AWS marketplace company's partnership lead's email and launch a campaign in 1 Claude Code prompt with @DeeplineCLI - first weekend helpdesk session!

How I did it:
1. Go to AWS Marketplace (or any listings page)
2. Open Chrome devtools → Network tab → Copy as cURL
3. Paste into Claude Code: "Get all alliance/partnership lead emails and create an Instantly campaign"

What Claude did next:
- Resolves company names → domains (free)
- Searches titles at each company, filters for partnership, alliances, ecosystem roles (free)
- Asks for approval
- Only THEN runs the paid email waterfall

Can replicate this flow for any website really. 

Comment DEEPLINE for $20 in credits to try this yourself across our 80+ integrations.

tweets.full_text:
Here's a quick summary:

The X post promotes a free 30-min Anthropic workshop (by the Claude Code creator) on mastering the tool. It calls it more valuable than $500 courses, noting most users miss ~40 commands. Video shows the presenter covering install (npm install -g anthropic-ai/claude-code), setup tweaks (/allowed-tools, context files like .claude.md), codebase Q&A prompts, team tool integration (logs, servers), and tips for richer context to boost smarts. Ends with SDK mention + Q&A.

The linked guide (quoted article) expands on best practices—watch + read for full value.

tweets.full_text:
cursor just got $10 billion in development funding from spacex.

here's why this is bigger than it looks:

cursor currently PAYS anthropic and openai to power its product.
that means every dollar cursor earns goes partially back to the companies trying to kill it with claude code and codex.

spacex brings colossus. 1 million H100-equivalent chips.
cursor now has the compute to train its own models and stop writing checks to its competitors.

the deal: spacex either pays $10B for the work or acquires cursor outright for $60B.

biggest software engineering acquisition in history. before spacex even IPOs.

the real war in AI isn't models vs models. it's who controls the compute and the distribution.

cursor just picked a side.

tweets.full_text:
Someone just built a Claude Code skill that strips every trace of AI from your writing.

It's called Humanizer. 

It detects 29 specific patterns from Wikipedia's "Signs of AI writing" guide, em dashes, rule of three, "it's not just X, it's Y", sycophantic openers, and rewrites your text to actually sound human.

100% Open Source.

tweets.full_text:
How to use:

git clone https://t.co/FlViwrkjlG
cd openclaw-claude-code
claude

CLAUDE.md acts as a routing hub — Claude Code reads only what's relevant instead of loading 20k lines into context.

Also available as an OpenClaw skill:
clawhub install openclaw-expert-guide

#OpenClaw #ClaudeCode #AI #OpenSource

user.name: Big Sheddy 🦅
user.description: i chase complex ideas and return with simple, honest notes | built @pullbase

quoted_status.full_text:
Lmao, this is too stressful. 

Simply ask codex the following: 

You are continuing a session from Claude Code.

Context:
- The previous session is stored locally in the `.claude/` directory.
- Locate the most recent session transcript (or the session titled "<session name>").
- Read the full transcript before taking any action.

Tasks:

1. Context Reconstruction
   - Summarize the goal of the session.
   - Identify what has already been completed.
   - Identify the exact stopping point.

2. Task Tracking
   - Extract any TODO list, checklist, or implicit tasks.
   - Classify each item:
     - DONE
     - PARTIALLY DONE (explain what’s missing)
     - NOT DONE

3. Continuation
   - Resume work from the last unfinished step.
   - Do NOT repeat completed work.
   - Follow the same approach, style, and decisions already established.

4. Execution Rules
   - Prefer continuing edits rather than restarting solutions.
   - If context is missing or ambiguous, inspect related files in the workspace.
   - Only ask questions if progress is blocked.

Output:
- Brief context summary
- Task status breakdown
- Clear next action
- Continue execution immediately

treating claude code as a team of specialists rather than one all-knowing assistant is what makes complex projects work. garry tan's gstack does it with named roles: designer, engineering manager, QA, each backed by a reusable skill. the agent doesn't guess what mode to be in. you tell it.

tweets.full_text:
A single, pure-Go binary. Embeds a BGE-int8 model (~35MB) so semantic search works offline. Works with any MCP client: Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Windsurf.

State lives in ~/.guild/*.db, locally, on your machine, not someone's cloud https://t.co/khpGbSQron
